items.title: What opens first
items.body: The app opens on the My Pills tab by default. The main navigation has exactly 3 tabs: My Pills, My Habits, and Settings.

items.title: What happens on launch
items.body: On the first app task, the app configures notification categories, loads Habit dashboard data, loads Pill dashboard data, refreshes the app badge, then runs a one-time startup health check in the background.

items.title: What happens when the app becomes active
items.body: Every time the app becomes active, both trackers run history reconciliation, refresh their dashboards, and reschedule notifications. This is the main lifecycle refresh point.

items.title: How screens open
items.body: Create, Details, and Edit screens for Habits and Pills open as sheets. They do not push deeper into the main tab navigation stack.

items.title: Device orientation
items.body: On iPhone, the app supports portrait orientation only. On iPad, the app supports portrait and landscape orientations.

items.title: Appearance modes
items.body: The app stores 3 appearance modes: System, Light, and Dark. The selected mode is applied at the app level.

items.title: App tint
items.body: Settings stores an app color: Blue, Indigo, Green, or Amber. Blue is the default app color and appears first in the palette. App colors apply to supported accent surfaces, while page backgrounds remain the system grouped background. Amber uses the iOS system orange color. Legacy stored tint values default, gray, yellow, cyan, teal, brown, red, and white are treated as Blue. Editable schedule checkmarks, Settings app-row icons, and Calendar Taken/Completed markers use the selected app color. Read-only schedule checkmarks, toggles, segmented picker selection, skipped markers, overdue and warning colors, card Edit/Info swipe actions, and Backup action rows remain fixed system colors.

items.title: Badge refresh cadence
items.body: The app refreshes badge state on launch, on store changes, and with a 60-second timer while the main content is active.

items.title: Empty and loading states
items.body: If Habits are loading, the screen shows a full-screen progress indicator. If loading is complete and there are no habits, the screen shows No habits yet with the message Create your first habit to start tracking your progress.

items.title: What a Habit card shows
items.body: Each Habit card shows the name, the current schedule summary, the current streak text, the reminder text if a reminder exists, and a status icon. If there is an active overdue scheduled day, the card shows a red Today, Yesterday, or date label such as 03 May 2026. Future Habits show Starts plus the start date. Archived Habits live on the separate Habit Archive page. Active overdue is not counted as a history gap. If recent history still needs review for another required scheduled day, the card also shows an amber warning icon. Otherwise, a green checkmark circle means completed today and a red xmark circle means skipped today.

items.title: Habit swipe actions
items.body: Leading swipe actions are used for day-state changes on active started Habits. Future and archived Habit cards do not expose day-state leading swipe actions. If today is already completed or skipped, the swipe action clears today's state with the arrow.uturn.backward system symbol. If today has no state, the swipe actions are Complete and Skip. Trailing swipe exposes Edit and Info. Edit uses system blue, Info uses system indigo, and Delete is not in card swipe actions.

items.title: Habit Archive page
items.body: My Habits has an Archive toolbar button. The Habit Archive page shows archived Habits without Build or Quit sections. Archived Habit cards do not expose day-state leading swipe actions, and Restore is handled from Edit.

items.title: Creating a Habit
items.body: Create Habit includes type, name, start date, reminder toggle, reminder time, Repeat, End Repeat, and End Date. Repeat opens a pushed editor with Days and Interval sections. Days supports weekday combinations. Interval is Every N days, limited to 2 through 5 days. Weekday summaries are Daily, Weekdays, Weekends, Weekly on Mon style labels for one selected weekday, or abbreviated day lists such as Mon, Wed, Fri for other combinations. Use schedule for history is no longer shown; new Habits use schedule-based history generation. The save button is enabled only when the habit name is not empty and the schedule rule is valid. If the 20-habit hard limit blocks creation, the error is shown as a dismissible floating warning banner.

items.title: Habit start date range
items.body: Habit start date can be selected from the last 5 years through the end of the second next calendar month. A future Habit remains in its normal Build or Quit dashboard section, shows Starts 03 May 2026 style dates, and has no today action/status, no overdue state, no notifications, and no history review before its start date.

items.title: Habit reminder permission behavior
items.body: If reminder is turned on during create or edit, the app requests notification authorization. If authorization is denied, reminder is turned back off and an alert offers to open iOS Settings. iOS only shows the system notification permission prompt once, so after Don't Allow the app cannot show that prompt again.

items.title: Habit details screen
items.body: Habit Details shows name, a Streaks block with current streak, best streak, and completed for, a read-only Schedule block with Reminder and Repeat, a History block with Start Date and End Date, and a read-only calendar. Repeat uses the same display style as dashboard cards and does not open a popover. The calendar shows a small tertiary system-gray dot under dates that match the effective schedule history. If any past scheduled day needs review, a dismissible floating warning banner appears. If the only missing day is the active overdue day, the banner says Finish updating overdue days. Otherwise it says Finish updating past days. The toolbar has Close on the left and Edit on the right. Edit is shown only when details load successfully.

items.title: Editing a Habit
items.body: Edit Habit shows name, a Schedule block with Reminder, Time, Repeat, End Repeat, and End Date, an editable calendar, and Delete plus Archive or Restore actions at the bottom. Start Date is not editable from Edit. Repeat opens the pushed Repeat editor. The calendar shows a small tertiary system-gray dot under dates that match the effective schedule history. Missing past-day review is shown as a dismissible floating warning banner that does not take space inside the calendar layout, and Save is disabled until it is resolved. A past active overdue day is included in Edit validation and uses overdue-specific warning copy when it is the only missing day. Delete uses a system alert with Cancel and Delete actions. Archive and Restore use system confirmation alerts.

items.title: How Habit history is stored
items.body: Habit history is stored as HabitCompletion rows. A day is completed when it has a positive completion source. A day is skipped when it has a non-positive source. A day is empty only when there is no row for that day.

items.title: Habit history modes
items.body: Habits store a history mode. Schedule-based means generated past history follows the selected schedule rule. Every-day means generated past history counts every day from the start date through yesterday. The selected mode is saved in the model and used later by create, details loading, backup, and restore. Dashboard cards exclude the active overdue day from history review, but Details and Edit include a past active overdue day. Missing scheduled editable past days are not auto-filled; they block saving until the user chooses a state. The current Details screen does not show a dedicated history mode row, and the Edit screen does not expose a history mode toggle.

items.title: Habit auto-fill on create
items.body: When a Habit is created, the repository generates completed history from the start date up to yesterday using schedule-based history generation. Only days matching the schedule rule are generated. Today is not prefilled.

items.title: Habit reconciliation
items.body: When the app becomes active or the active timer refreshes, Habits refresh derived overdue and history-review state. Reconciliation does not backfill missing Habit history and does not auto-skip overdue days. The latest due scheduled day becomes active overdue only while it is empty; older empty due scheduled days stay as history gaps until the user fixes them in Edit.

items.title: Habit editable history window
items.body: Habit edit history is limited to the last 30 days and never goes earlier than the habit start date. Older history can still be visible in read-only details, but it is not editable from the edit screen.

items.title: Today versus past days in Habit edit
items.body: Today can still be empty, completed, or skipped. Every past editable scheduled Habit day must be Completed or Skipped before saving, including a past active overdue day. Saving Edit Habit does not auto-fill missing past scheduled days; if any past editable scheduled day is empty, the app shows a dismissible floating warning banner without listing dates and disables Save until the user chooses the state. Non-scheduled editable days may still be marked manually and count when marked. If the schedule is changed, the new schedule version resolves internally from today for active items, or from Start Date for future items.

items.title: Current streak
items.body: Current streak increases on completed days. A missing unscheduled day does not reset it. A missed scheduled day in the past resets it. Today does not reset the streak just because it is not completed yet.

items.title: Best streak and completed total
items.body: Best streak is the longest streak found by the same streak logic. Completed for is the total count of unique completed days stored in history.

items.title: Empty and loading states
items.body: If Pills are loading, the screen shows a full-screen progress indicator. If loading is complete and there are no pills, the screen shows No pills yet with the message Create your first pill to track dosage, reminders, and taken days.

items.title: How Pill cards are grouped
items.body: The Pill dashboard is split into exactly 2 sections: Today and Pending. Today contains pills scheduled for today. Pending contains the rest.

items.title: What a Pill card shows
items.body: Each Pill card shows the name, dosage, schedule summary, total taken days, reminder text if a reminder exists, and a status icon. If there is an active overdue scheduled day, the card shows a red Today, Yesterday, or date label such as 03 May 2026. Future Pills appear in Pending and show Starts plus the start date. Archived Pills live on the separate Pill Archive page. Active overdue is not counted as a history gap. If recent history still needs review for another required scheduled day, the card also shows an amber warning icon. Otherwise, a green checkmark circle means taken today and a red xmark circle means skipped today.

items.title: Pill swipe actions
items.body: Leading swipe actions are used for day-state changes on active started Pills. Future and archived Pill cards do not expose day-state leading swipe actions. If today is already taken or skipped, the swipe action clears today's state with the arrow.uturn.backward system symbol. If today has no state, the swipe actions are Taken and Skip. Trailing swipe exposes Edit and Info. Edit uses system blue, Info uses system indigo, and Delete is not in card swipe actions.

items.title: Pill Archive page
items.body: My Pills has an Archive toolbar button. The Pill Archive page shows archived Pills without Today or Pending sections. Archived Pill cards do not expose day-state leading swipe actions, and Restore is handled from Edit.

items.title: Creating a Pill
items.body: Create Pill includes name, dosage, start date, reminder toggle, reminder time, Repeat, End Date, and an optional description. Repeat opens a pushed editor with Days and Interval sections. Days supports weekday combinations. Interval includes Never for one-time Pills and Every N days, limited to 2 through 5 days. Weekday summaries are Daily, Weekdays, Weekends, Weekly on Mon style labels for one selected weekday, or abbreviated day lists such as Mon, Wed, Fri for other combinations. Use schedule for history is no longer shown; new Pills use schedule-based history generation. Save is enabled only when the pill name is not empty, the dosage is not empty, and the schedule rule is valid. If the 20-pill hard limit blocks creation, the error is shown as a dismissible floating warning banner.

items.title: Pill start date range
items.body: Pill start date can be selected from the last 5 years through the end of the second next calendar month. A future Pill appears in Pending and shows Starts 03 May 2026 style dates, but it has no today action/status, no overdue state, no notifications, and no history review before its start date.

items.title: Pill details screen
items.body: Pill Details shows name, dosage, a Streak block with Taken for, a read-only Schedule block with Reminder and Repeat, a History block with Start Date and End Date, a read-only calendar, and description if one exists. Repeat uses the same display style as dashboard cards and does not open a popover. The calendar shows a small tertiary system-gray dot under dates that match the effective schedule history. If any past scheduled day needs review, a dismissible floating warning banner appears. If the only missing day is the active overdue day, the banner says Finish updating overdue days. Otherwise it says Finish updating past days. The toolbar has Close on the left and Edit on the right. Edit is shown only when details load successfully.

items.title: Editing a Pill
items.body: Edit Pill shows name, dosage, a Schedule block with Reminder, Time, Repeat, and End Date, an editable calendar, description, and Delete plus Archive or Restore actions at the bottom. Start Date is not editable from Edit. Repeat opens the pushed Repeat editor. The calendar shows a small tertiary system-gray dot under dates that match the effective schedule history. Missing past-day review is shown as a dismissible floating warning banner that does not take space inside the calendar layout, and Save is disabled until it is resolved. A past active overdue day is included in Edit validation and uses overdue-specific warning copy when it is the only missing day. Delete uses a system alert with Cancel and Delete actions. Archive and Restore use system confirmation alerts.

items.title: How Pill history is stored
items.body: Pill history is stored as PillIntake rows. A day is taken when it has a positive intake source. A day is skipped when it has a skipped source. A day is empty only when there is no row for that day.

items.title: Pill history modes
items.body: Pills store a history mode. Schedule-based means generated past history follows the selected schedule rule. Every-day means generated past history counts every day from the start date through yesterday. The selected mode is saved and used later by create, details loading, backup, and restore. Dashboard cards exclude the active overdue day from history review, but Details and Edit include a past active overdue day. Missing scheduled editable past days are not auto-filled; they block saving until the user chooses a state. The current Details screen does not show a dedicated history mode row, and the Edit screen does not expose a history mode toggle.

items.title: Pill prefilled taken history on create
items.body: When a new Pill is created, the repository generates taken history from the start date up to yesterday using schedule-based history generation. Only days matching the schedule rule are generated.

items.title: What repository create does for Pills
items.body: When the Pill is saved, the repository inserts manualEdit intake rows for every generated taken day. Today is not prefilled.

items.title: Pill reconciliation
items.body: When the app becomes active or the active timer refreshes, Pills refresh derived overdue and history-review state. Reconciliation does not backfill missing Pill history and does not auto-skip overdue days. The latest due scheduled day becomes active overdue only while it is empty; older empty due scheduled days stay as history gaps until the user fixes them in Edit.

items.title: Pill editable history window
items.body: Pill edit history is limited to the last 30 days and never goes earlier than the pill start date. Older history can still be visible in read-only details, but it is not editable from the edit screen.

items.title: Today versus past days in Pill edit
items.body: Today can still be empty, taken, or skipped. Every past editable scheduled Pill day must be Taken or Skipped before saving, including a past active overdue day. Saving Edit Pill does not auto-fill missing past scheduled days; if any past editable scheduled day is empty, the app shows a dismissible floating warning banner without listing dates and disables Save until the user chooses the state. Non-scheduled editable days may still be marked manually and count when marked. If the schedule is changed, the new schedule version resolves internally from today for active items, or from Start Date for future items.

items.title: Pill totals
items.body: Pills do not use streaks. Taken for is the total count of unique taken days stored in history.

items.title: Notification categories
items.body: Habits use actions Mark as Completed and Mark as Skipped. Pills use Mark as Taken, Mark as Skipped, and Remind me in 10 mins.

items.title: Scheduling window
items.body: The app schedules reminder notifications only for the next 2 days. It does not build an unlimited long-range pending queue.

items.title: Grouped reminders
items.body: If fewer than 3 reminders land on the same scheduled time, the app creates individual reminders. If 3 or more reminders land on the same scheduled time, the app creates a single aggregated reminder for that time.

items.title: When reminders are eligible
items.body: A reminder candidate is created only when reminder is enabled, the item is not archived, the local day is not before the item start date, the local day is not after the final scheduled day when End Date exists, the local day matches the current schedule rule, the day is not already completed or taken, the day is not already skipped, and the scheduled reminder time is still in the future. Reminder planning intentionally scans the next 2 local days; this is currently accepted for sparse weekly and interval schedules because the expected usage pattern is daily app opens, which trigger a fresh reschedule.

items.title: What tapping a reminder does
items.body: Default tapping an individual or aggregated Habit reminder opens My Habits. Default tapping an individual or aggregated Pill reminder opens My Pills.

items.title: How day resolution works
items.body: Reminder actions do not trust the delivery timestamp as the only source of truth. They use localDate from the notification payload first, and only fall back to the notification date if localDate is missing or invalid.

items.title: Pill remind later
items.body: Remind me in 10 mins creates a separate delayed Pill reminder using minute-level calendar precision. Because seconds are intentionally not preserved, the delayed reminder may fire up to 59 seconds earlier than an exact second-level 10-minute delay; this is accepted by customer requirements. The delayed reminder keeps the original pillID and localDate in its payload.

items.title: Pill remind later persistence
items.body: Snoozed Pill reminders are kept separate from regular scheduled reminders. A global Pill reschedule removes only regular Pill reminders and leaves active remind-later reminders in place. Snoozed reminders are removed when the same pill and day are taken or skipped, or when the pill is deleted.

items.title: What the badge counts
items.body: The app badge is the total number of overdue Habits plus overdue Pills.

items.title: When a Habit is overdue
items.body: A Habit is overdue when it is not archived and the latest due scheduled day is empty. Scheduled days become due at reminder time, or at 00:00 when reminders are disabled. Older empty due scheduled days are history gaps, not active overdue.

items.title: When a Pill is overdue
items.body: A Pill is overdue when it is not archived and the latest due scheduled day is empty. Scheduled days become due at reminder time, or at 00:00 when reminders are disabled. Older empty due scheduled days are history gaps, not active overdue.

items.title: Projected badge count in notifications
items.body: When the app builds reminder notifications, it also calculates a projected overdue badge count for the exact scheduled reminder time of each notification request.

items.title: What overdue does not do
items.body: Badge calculation is derived state only. Reconciliation does not persist skipped rows for overdue catch-up.

items.title: What overdue does not change
items.body: When a newer scheduled due day appears, older empty due scheduled days become history gaps. They remain manual review and can only be fixed in Edit.

items.title: End Date and archive
items.body: End Date is optional for both Pills and Habits. If a date is selected, the final active scheduled day is the last scheduled day on or before that date. Once that final day is taken/completed or skipped, the item archives automatically without confirmation. If the final day remains empty, the item stays active and can become overdue. Repeat Never for Pills is a one-time schedule on Start Date and archives automatically after that day is taken or skipped.

items.title: What Backup screen shows
items.body: Backup shows Last backup, Total size, and Folder. Last backup uses the same color as its cloud status icon, green when a readable backup exists and red when it does not, and dates use 03 May at 22:35 style formatting. Create Backup and Restore Backup are full-width capsule buttons; Create uses the primary label color and Restore stays system red. Create and Restore confirmations use system alerts instead of popover confirmation dialogs; alert actions use short labels: Backup and Restore. Backup feedback uses dismissible floating banners that auto-hide after 4 seconds or clear when leaving the Backup screen. The Home Screen app icon exposes a Create Backup quick action; choosing it opens Settings > Backup only, and backup creation still starts only after the user taps Create Backup on the Backup screen.

items.title: Folder with an existing backup
items.body: Choosing a folder never restores data automatically. Backup action notices are derived from the selected folder state and remembered backup fingerprints, not from a temporary screen session. If the folder contains a readable backup that has not been created or restored by this app install, the Backup screen shows Backup available. Restore when ready. as a blue floating informational banner and disables Create Backup. If the same backup was already created or restored by this app install, no restore-needed informational banner is shown.

items.title: Folder without a backup
items.body: If the selected folder has no readable backup, Backup shows No backup found. Create one to get started. as a blue floating informational banner. Create Backup stays available and Restore Backup is disabled.

items.title: Unreadable backup files
items.body: If backup files exist but cannot be read, Backup shows Backup can’t be read. Choose another location or create a new one. as a red floating banner. Restore Backup is disabled. Backup Created and Restore Complete are green floating success banners.

items.title: Backup file names
items.body: The main backup file is LoonyBear.json.gz. If a previous main backup exists, it is moved to LoonyBear.previous.json.gz before a new main backup is written.

items.title: What backup includes
items.body: Backup includes Habits, Habit schedule rules, Habit completions, Habit ordering, Pills, Pill schedule rules, Pill intakes, reminder settings, stored history modes, optional End Date values, archived state for both trackers, and app appearance settings.

items.title: Restore safety snapshot
items.body: Before restore, the app tries to create a restore snapshot file named LoonyBear.restore-snapshot.json.gz. If snapshot payload creation fails because the local store is already corrupted, restore can still continue. If snapshot encoding, compression, or writing fails, restore is aborted.

items.title: Restore validation
items.body: Restore validates schema version, app appearance settings when present, history mode values, schedule rules including interval and one-time kinds, source values, and duplicate identifiers before replacing the store.

items.title: What restore does after success
items.body: After a successful restore, the app resets the read context, applies backed-up theme mode and app tint when present, refreshes dashboards, and rebuilds Habit and Pill notifications from restored data. Legacy backups without app settings keep the current theme and tint.

items.title: Folder access
items.body: Backup uses a stored security-scoped folder bookmark. If the bookmark is no longer valid, the Backup screen asks the user to choose the folder again before backup or restore can continue.

items.title: Settings sections
items.body: Settings contains an Appearance card with System, Light, and Dark mode plus the app color palette; an App card with Backup and Rules & Logic; and an About card. About shows the current app version and build.

items.title: What Rules & Logic is
items.body: Rules & Logic is a local reference screen loaded from RulesLogicContent.json inside the app bundle. If the file cannot be loaded or decoded, the screen shows Rules & Logic unavailable.

items.title: Version footer
items.body: Settings shows Version <short version> and Build <build number> from the app bundle metadata. Build numbers are auto-incremented from CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION during each build and displayed as a six-digit value, for example 001025.

items.title: Top-level composition
items.body: The app is composed from LoonyBearApp, AppEnvironment, ContentView, and RootTabView. AppEnvironment builds HabitAppState, PillAppState, NotificationService, PillNotificationService, AppBadgeService, AppNotificationCoordinator, and WidgetSyncService dependencies.

items.title: Main state holders
items.body: HabitAppState owns Habit dashboard loading, create, update, delete, daily mutations, detail state loading, reconciliation trigger, and Habit notification preparation. PillAppState owns the same responsibilities for Pills, plus Pill reordering.

items.title: Storage architecture
items.body: All persisted application data is stored in Core Data. Habits and Pills use separate repositories: CoreDataHabitRepository and CoreDataPillRepository. Both repositories use a shared CoreDataRepositoryContext for background writes and read-context refresh.

items.title: Screen model
items.body: The app uses a tab root with sheet-based create, details, and edit flows. The default tab is My Pills. Sheets are identified by enum state carrying either a fixed mode or a UUID.

items.title: Calendar navigation
items.body: Shared month calendars use header chevron buttons for month navigation. Horizontal swipe paging is disabled on Details and Edit calendar surfaces. Calendar blocks keep a stable six-week footprint and adjust vertical row spacing for shorter months. Habit and Pill Details/Edit calendar day views show a small tertiary system-gray dot under dates that match the effective schedule history.

items.title: Repeat editor
items.body: Create and Edit screens open Repeat as a pushed screen inside the current sheet. The screen has Days and Interval sections. Days shows full weekday names with checkmarks and prevents leaving the schedule empty. Interval uses Every N days [Stepper] with a native Stepper, limited to 2 through 5 days, and for Pills only also includes Never. Details screens show Repeat as read-only text and do not open a schedule picker.

items.title: Settings navigation persistence
items.body: Settings uses route-based navigation for Backup and Rules & Logic. Settings child screens keep the custom tinted back button and preserve the native left-edge swipe-back gesture. The selected tab and active Settings route are stored in scene storage so restore-driven appearance changes and tint rebuilds do not drop the user out of the Backup screen.

items.title: Habit model types
items.body: HabitType has exactly 2 values: build and quit. CompletionSource has exactly 6 values: swipe, manual edit, notification, restore, auto fill, and skipped. HabitHistoryMode has exactly 2 values: scheduleBased and everyDay.

items.title: Pill model types
items.body: PillCompletionSource has exactly 5 values: swipe, manual edit, notification, restore, and skipped. PillHistoryMode has exactly 2 values: scheduleBased and everyDay.

items.title: Reminder time rules
items.body: ReminderTime stores hour and minute as integers. ReminderTime.default rounds the current time up to a 5-minute boundary. If rounding reaches 60 minutes, the hour is incremented and minute becomes 0. Create/Edit reminder time rows use the native compact system time picker. Create Start Date rows use the native compact system date picker. End Repeat option popover transitions briefly block neighboring compact pickers to avoid overlapping UIKit presentations. Edit screens do not expose Start Date.

items.title: Schedule model
items.body: ScheduleRule stores weekday selections as weekly WeekdaySet bitmasks, Every N days as intervalDays, or Never repeat as a one-time Pill rule. Weekday summaries are labels derived from the selected days: Daily for Monday through Sunday, Weekdays for Monday through Friday, Weekends for Saturday and Sunday, Weekly on Mon style labels for one selected weekday, and abbreviated day lists such as Mon, Wed, Fri for other weekday combinations. IntervalDays is limited to 2 through 5 days. Initial interval schedules are anchored to the item start date; edited interval schedules are anchored to the active schedule version effectiveFrom. Legacy daily, weekdays, and weekends schedule kind values are read back as weekly masks.

items.title: Persistence container
items.body: The persistent container name is LoonyBear. Persistent store descriptions enable automatic lightweight migration and inferred mapping models so additive model changes can migrate existing local stores. The main viewContext automatically merges parent changes and uses NSMergeByPropertyObjectTrumpMergePolicy. Background contexts use the same merge policy.

items.title: Read and write coordination
items.body: Repositories perform writes through CoreDataRepositoryContext.performWrite. After a successful write, the read context is refreshed with refreshAllObjects. If the read context is main queue and the current thread is already main, refreshAllObjects is called directly.

items.title: Editable history window
items.body: EditableHistoryWindow.dates returns up to 30 days ending at today, never earlier than startDate. EditableHistoryWindow.pastDates returns the same window without today.

items.title: Past day normalization
items.body: EditableHistoryContract.normalizedSelection normalizes all day sets to start-of-day values and fills missing required past days using either positive or skipped default selection. Any skipped day that is also positive is removed from the skipped set.

items.title: Schedule applicability
items.body: HistoryScheduleApplicability can compute past editable days, past scheduled editable days, past required editable days, and scheduled calendar indicator days. Effective schedule for a given day is the latest schedule version whose effectiveFrom is not later than that day, ordered by effectiveFrom, version, and createdAt. Weekly rules use weekday membership. Every-N-days interval rules use the active schedule version effectiveFrom as the interval anchor. Edit schedule changes use today as effectiveFrom for active items and Start Date as effectiveFrom for future items; the calendar preview drops replaced future schedule versions so dots match the post-save schedule.

items.title: Create Habit validations
items.body: CreateHabitUseCase requires a non-empty trimmed name and a valid schedule rule. CoreDataHabitRepository also enforces a hard limit of 20 habits. Create Habit presents hard-limit errors with the shared dismissible floating warning banner.

items.title: Habit create storage
items.body: Habit creation inserts a Habit row, inserts the first HabitScheduleVersion row, stores schedule-based historyModeRaw, stores optional End Date, sets isArchived to false, and inserts one HabitCompletion per generated initial completed day from startDate through yesterday.

items.title: Habit create autofill source
items.body: Habit create history generation inserts HabitCompletion rows with sourceRaw equal to auto fill. Only days matching the schedule rule are generated. Today is not prefilled.

items.title: Habit update behavior
items.body: Habit update changes name, reminder fields, optional End Date, and optionally inserts a new HabitScheduleVersion if the schedule rule changed. The new schedule version starts from today for active items, or from Start Date for future items. Editable history is normalized and written back day by day using manual edit or skipped sources. Update validation requires every past editable scheduled day to be Completed or Skipped; missing scheduled past states block save instead of being auto-filled. Manual Restore saves the current edit form before moving the Habit back to its active section.

items.title: Habit today actions
items.body: completeHabitToday creates a swipe completion or converts an existing skipped row to swipe. skipHabitToday inserts skipped if no row exists and leaves already skipped rows unchanged. clearHabitDayStateToday deletes today's row if it exists. When a card has an active overdue day, card swipe actions apply to that overdue day instead of today.

items.title: Habit reconciliation behavior
items.body: Habit reconciliation is a no-op for stored history rows. It does not auto-fill missing past Habit history rows and does not auto-skip older overdue days. Dashboard and badge state are derived from the latest due scheduled day, while older missing scheduled days stay in manual review.

items.title: Create Pill validations
items.body: Create Pill requires a non-empty trimmed name, a non-empty trimmed dosage, and a valid schedule rule. CoreDataPillRepository also enforces a hard limit of 20 pills. Create Pill presents hard-limit errors with the shared dismissible floating warning banner.

items.title: Create Pill day generation
items.body: During repository create, initial takenDays are generated from startDate through yesterday. Generation includes only days matching the schedule rule. Today is not prefilled.

items.title: Pill create storage
items.body: Pill creation inserts a Pill row, inserts the first PillScheduleVersion row, stores schedule-based historyModeRaw, stores optional End Date, sets isArchived to false, and inserts one PillIntake per generated taken day with sourceRaw manual edit. Today is not prefilled.

items.title: Pill update behavior
items.body: Pill update changes name, dosage, details, reminder fields, optional End Date, and optionally inserts a new PillScheduleVersion if the schedule rule changed. The new schedule version starts from today for active items, or from Start Date for future items. Editable history is normalized and written back day by day using manual edit or skipped sources. Update validation requires every past editable scheduled day to be Taken or Skipped; missing scheduled past states block save instead of being auto-filled. Manual Restore saves the current edit form before moving the Pill back to its active section.

items.title: Pill today actions
items.body: markTakenToday creates a swipe intake or converts an existing skipped row to swipe. skipPillToday inserts skipped if no row exists and leaves already skipped rows unchanged. clearPillDayStateToday deletes today's row if it exists. When a card has an active overdue day, card swipe actions apply to that overdue day instead of today.

items.title: Pill reconciliation behavior
items.body: Pill reconciliation is a no-op for stored history rows. It does not backfill missing past Pill history rows and does not auto-skip older overdue days. Dashboard and badge state are derived from the latest due scheduled day, while older missing scheduled days stay in manual review.

items.title: Shared notification helpers
items.body: NotificationSupport defines Notification.Name keys for store changes and tab opening, a NotificationStoreContext for Core Data notification reads and writes, local notification authorization helpers, stale delivered cleanup, pending request removal helpers, and localDate identifier encoding and parsing.

items.title: Habit notification constants
items.body: Habit notifications use categoryIdentifier habit.reminder, summaryCategoryIdentifier habit.reminder.summary, action identifiers habit.complete and habit.skip, schedulingWindowDays 2, and aggregationThreshold 3. The 2-day planning window is accepted for now for both weekly and interval schedules because lifecycle refreshes are expected daily.

items.title: Pill notification constants
items.body: Pill notifications use categoryIdentifier pill.reminder, summaryCategoryIdentifier pill.reminder.summary, action identifiers pill.take, pill.skip, and pill.remind_later, schedulingWindowDays 2, aggregationThreshold 3, and remindLaterInterval 600 seconds with accepted minute-level trigger precision. The 2-day planning window is accepted for now for both weekly and interval schedules because lifecycle refreshes are expected daily.

items.title: Reminder candidate rules
items.body: Both Habit and Pill reminder services build candidates only when reminder is enabled, reminderTime exists, the item is not archived, the local day is not before startDate, the local day is not after the final scheduled day when End Date exists, the local day matches the active schedule rule, the local day is not already completed or taken, the local day is not skipped, and the scheduled reminder time is still in the future.

items.title: Default tap routing
items.body: Habit default taps route aggregated and individual Habit notifications to My Habits. Pill default taps route pill_aggregated and pill notifications to My Pills.

items.title: Badge calculation
items.body: AppBadgeService calculates overdue Habits and overdue Pills separately and sums them. Overdue means activeOverdueDay is set from the latest due scheduled day while that day is empty. Older history gaps are not badge-counted overdue. Dashboard cards do not count active overdue as a history review gap while it remains actionable; Details and Edit still surface a past active overdue day as requiring review.

items.title: Backup schema and files
items.body: Backup schemaVersion is 1. The main backup file is LoonyBear.json.gz. The previous backup file is LoonyBear.previous.json.gz. The restore snapshot file is LoonyBear.restore-snapshot.json.gz. New builds can restore older v1 backups that do not contain scheduleKind, intervalDays, End Date, or archived state. New interval and archive payloads still use schemaVersion 1 by product decision; restoring new backups into older app builds is not supported.

items.title: Backup payload contents
items.body: BackupArchive contains habits with End Date and archived state, habit schedule versions with schedule kind, weekday mask, and interval days, habit completion records, habit ordering, optional app settings, pills with End Date and archived state, pill schedule versions with schedule kind, weekday mask, and interval days, and pill intake records. Schedule kind can represent weekly, custom interval days, or one-time Pill rules. BackupHabit and BackupPill both carry stored historyMode values. BackupAppSettings carries appearanceMode and appTint.

items.title: Backup file state
items.body: BackupStatus includes a fileState: none, available, created, restored, or unreadable. Readable backup files are fingerprinted from their compressed data using SHA-256. Successful create stores the last created fingerprint; successful restore stores the last restored fingerprint.

items.title: Restore safety and validation
items.body: Restore first attempts to create a compressed snapshot of the current store. If snapshot payload creation fails with a DataIntegrityError, restore continues. If snapshot encoding, compression, or file writing fails, restore aborts. Restore validates schema version, app settings when present, history modes, reminder times, schedule rules including interval and one-time kinds, source values, foreign key references, and duplicate identifiers before replacing the store.

items.title: Reliability layer
items.body: ReliabilitySupport defines DataIntegrityIssue, DataIntegrityReport, DataIntegrityError, IntegrityReportBuilder, ReminderValidation, WeekdayValidation, and ReliabilityLog. DataIntegrityError reports the operation name and the count of corrupted records detected.

items.title: Compression service
items.body: CompressionService performs gzip compression and decompression using zlib. The two failure cases exposed by the service are compressionFailed and decompressionFailed.

items.title: Widget snapshot system
items.body: WidgetSyncService writes a WidgetSnapshot built from the Habit dashboard only. It skips unchanged content, writes on a utility queue, retries the same content if a previous save failed, and reloads all widget timelines only after a successful changed save. WidgetSnapshotStore saves into app group group.com.valr4k.LoonyBear when available, otherwise into Application Support under LoonyBear/widget-snapshot.json.
